description: Frame the email like a Snapchat story: a sequence of full-bleed 'snaps' that reveal the offer, capped with a streak counter that rewards repeat opens or purchases.
whyItWorks: The reveal mechanic drives scroll depth, and the 'this disappears' framing manufactures urgency without a literal countdown timer.
ecomTieIn: Use a streak counter to reward repeat buyers — every order keeps the streak (and the discount) alive.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for a marketing email in a Snapchat-story world. Use full-bleed vertical phone-story composition, playful overlay captions, snap sticker elements, a streak counter, subtle doodles and a central fashion product moment. Make it ecommerce-ready, mobile-first, high contrast, with space for headline and CTA. Do not use real Snapchat logos or protected UI exactly; create a playful inspired interface.

description: Build the email as an Instagram profile feed: grid tiles tease benefits, story highlights become product categories, and a 'new post' hero launches the drop.
whyItWorks: Customers already understand the visual grammar, so they can scan quickly; the grid makes multiple products feel curated rather than cluttered.
ecomTieIn: Turn story highlight bubbles into shoppable categories such as New In, Bestsellers, Personalised and Gifts.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by an Instagram profile feed. Use a clean grid of product tiles, circular story-highlight icons, a bold new-post hero tile and social-feed visual cues without copying Instagram branding. Make it stylish, mobile-first, product-led, with space for headline and CTA.

description: Turn the email into a scrolling trend: quick-cut product benefits, comment-style objections, and a 'sound on' hook that frames the launch as something people are already talking about.
whyItWorks: Fast visual rhythm suits mobile and makes static email blocks feel more kinetic and social.
ecomTieIn: Use comment replies to answer buying objections: fit, fabric, sizing, delivery, returns.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email in a TikTok-inspired trend world. Use vertical video-card framing, comment bubbles, reaction stickers, fast-cut product moments and a bold 'sound on' style hook. Do not copy TikTok logos or exact UI. Make it fashion ecommerce, mobile-first, energetic and clear.

description: Lay the email out like a curated Pinterest board, with pinned outfits, gifting ideas and product close-ups clustered by mood.
whyItWorks: Pinterest is planning-led, so it works brilliantly for gifting, outfits, interiors and collections people want to save or return to.
ecomTieIn: Create shoppable boards such as Weekend Uniform, Giftable Favourites, Back In Stock or Colour Crush.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a curated Pinterest board. Use overlapping pinned cards, outfit inspiration tiles, product close-ups, paper textures, soft shadows and mood-board composition. Keep it elegant, shoppable, mobile-first and brand-led. Do not copy Pinterest branding.

description: Present the product as a cheeky marketplace listing, complete with condition, location, seller notes and 'is this still available?' jokes.
whyItWorks: It creates instant recognition and humour while making product details feel native rather than dry.
ecomTieIn: Use listing fields for real product details: condition = brand new; location = made in the UK; seller notes = personalised by us.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a social marketplace listing. Use listing-card layout, seller notes, product photo area, condition badges, price-tag styling and conversational message snippets. Make it funny, ecommerce-ready and mobile-first. Do not copy Facebook branding or UI exactly.

description: Use a dual-camera layout: the polished product shot paired with the messy real-life reason the customer needs it.
whyItWorks: The contrast creates honesty, relatability and humour, perfect for brands that do not want to sound over-produced.
ecomTieIn: Show ideal use vs real use: perfect outfit shot beside school-run chaos, packing panic, coffee spill or gift-wrap mess.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a BeReal dual-camera moment. Show a polished fashion product shot alongside a messy real-life snapshot, with candid humour and clear product focus. Make it mobile-first, warm, relatable and ecommerce-ready. Do not copy BeReal branding.

description: Make the email look like an absurdly polished professional announcement for something deeply unserious: a promotion, achievement or 'career milestone' tied to the product.
whyItWorks: The contrast between corporate language and silly ecommerce creates dry humour and strong shareability.
ecomTieIn: Use mock job titles, endorsements or achievement badges to sell personality-led products: Chief Biscuit Officer, Director of Coffee Runs, Head of Saying No.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a corporate social announcement. Use a polished professional profile-card layout, mock achievement badge, formal announcement styling and a fashion product presented like a career milestone. Make it witty, ecommerce-ready and mobile-first. Do not use LinkedIn branding.

description: Structure the email as a short viral thread, with each block revealing one punchy reason to care about the product.
whyItWorks: Threads create momentum and make product education feel fast, opinionated and easy to skim.
ecomTieIn: Use each 'reply' to tackle one selling point: fit, colour, giftability, offer, urgency.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a viral social thread. Use stacked post cards, reply lines, short punchy text zones and clear product imagery. Make it mobile-first, witty and easy to skim. Do not use Twitter/X branding or exact UI.

description: Turn the email into an AMA where the product answers brutally honest customer questions about fit, use, sizing or whether they really need another one.
whyItWorks: The format gives permission to handle objections directly while keeping the tone witty and conversational.
ecomTieIn: Use upvoted questions as buying objections, then answer each with product proof, reviews or images.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a Reddit AMA thread. Use Q&A cards, upvote-style arrows, honest customer questions, product proof areas and a witty conversational tone. Make it mobile-first, clear and ecommerce-ready. Do not copy Reddit branding or exact UI.

description: Design the email hero like a dramatic YouTube thumbnail: big face or product reaction, bold claim, arrows, circles and an implied 'you need to see this' reveal.
whyItWorks: Thumbnail language is engineered for clicks, so it works well for hero sections where the job is immediate attention.
ecomTieIn: Use video-style hooks for product launches: We Tried It, You Asked For This, The Restock Everyone Wanted.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a dramatic YouTube thumbnail. Use bold headline text, expressive reaction, product cut-out, arrows, circles and high-contrast composition. Make it fun, click-worthy, mobile-first and product-led. Do not use YouTube branding.

description: Frame the email as an invite-only Close Friends drop, using private-circle language, green accents and 'you saw it first' messaging.
whyItWorks: It creates intimacy and exclusivity without needing a heavy discount.
ecomTieIn: Use it for VIP early access, limited runs or email-subscriber-only perks.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by an Instagram Close Friends-style private drop. Use intimate invite-only framing, green accent circle details, product reveal, private-access stickers and stylish ecommerce composition. Do not copy Instagram branding or UI exactly.

description: Turn the email into an exaggerated product listing: star rating, feature bullets, delivery badge, customer Q&A and 'frequently bought together'.
whyItWorks: It borrows high-converting ecommerce patterns customers already know, but the playful framing makes it feel more branded than transactional.
ecomTieIn: Use 'frequently bought together' to drive AOV and cross-sell bundles.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a marketplace product listing. Use a large product image, star-rating motif, feature bullet blocks, delivery badge, frequently-bought-together strip and clear CTA area. Do not copy Amazon branding or exact layout; make it playful and brand-owned.

description: Make the email feel like a home-shopping segment: host callouts, price graphics, limited quantity messaging and 'operators standing by' energy.
whyItWorks: QVC is inherently conversion-led; it makes urgency, explanation and demo feel entertaining rather than pushy.
ecomTieIn: Use presenter-style callouts to explain features, fabric, size, personalisation or bundle value.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a retro QVC home-shopping segment. Use presenter callout graphics, product demonstration framing, price badge, limited-quantity ticker, studio lighting and bold CTA space. Make it fun, conversion-led and mobile-first. Do not use QVC branding.

description: Design the email as if the customer is already at checkout, with the product in basket, progress bar nearly complete and one final nudge.
whyItWorks: It reduces decision friction by framing purchase as the obvious next step, not a new decision.
ecomTieIn: Great for abandoned browse, cart recovery, urgency and back-in-stock emails.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a checkout page. Use basket summary cards, nearly-complete progress bar, product image, delivery note, secure-checkout cues and a large final CTA. Make it clean, mobile-first and conversion-led. Do not copy any real checkout UI exactly.

description: Make the email look like a receipt that itemises reasons to buy, savings, perks or emotional benefits.
whyItWorks: The receipt format is naturally scannable and lets you quantify value in a playful way.
ecomTieIn: Use line items for product benefits, bundle savings, free delivery threshold and limited extras.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a playful shopping receipt. Use itemised line rows, subtotal-style value stack, product image clipped beside the receipt, stamp marks, paper texture and a clear CTA. Make it mobile-first, readable and brand-led.

description: Create a tear-out coupon book where each section unlocks an offer, free gift, bundle or perk.
whyItWorks: Coupons create tactile value and encourage customers to scroll through multiple offers without feeling overwhelmed.
ecomTieIn: Use detachable coupon blocks for spend thresholds, category discounts or loyalty perks.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a retro coupon book. Use perforated coupon edges, tear-off tabs, offer blocks, product cut-outs, vintage print texture and a bold CTA strip. Make it ecommerce-ready, mobile-first, readable and playful.

description: Frame the product as an instruction manual: how to wear it, gift it, style it, wash it or justify buying it.
whyItWorks: Manuals turn product education into a format people instantly understand, making detail-heavy emails easier to digest.
ecomTieIn: Use diagrams for fit, styling, personalisation options, sizing and care instructions.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a playful product instruction manual. Use diagram labels, numbered steps, product cutaway or flat-lay, arrows, fit notes, paper texture and clear CTA area. Make it mobile-first, useful and visually clean.

description: Build the email as a guided shopping assistant with gift categories, recipient types and 'if they like X, buy Y' logic.
whyItWorks: It removes choice paralysis and is especially useful for seasonal, gifting or multi-product emails.
ecomTieIn: Create recipient-led modules: For the book lover, for the new mum, for the impossible-to-buy-for friend.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a gift guide. Use recipient category cards, product tiles, ribbons, tags, decision-path arrows and a warm editorial shopping-assistant feel. Make it mobile-first, shoppable and easy to scan.

description: Make the email look like a stamped loyalty card, with rewards, milestones or perks marked off visually.
whyItWorks: Progress and completion cues make customers more likely to act, especially when they feel close to a reward.
ecomTieIn: Use stamps for order milestones, VIP perks, free delivery thresholds or repeat purchase rewards.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a stamped loyalty card. Use punch-card layout, reward stamps, progress markers, product cut-out, paper texture and a clear CTA. Make it warm, playful, mobile-first and ecommerce-ready.

description: Create a dramatic shop-floor doorbuster email with signage, stacks, arrows, timers and limited-stock energy.
whyItWorks: It leans into urgency and retail theatre, making discounts feel event-like rather than apologetic.
ecomTieIn: Use for seasonal sale launches, bundle offers and time-limited stock clearances.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email styled as a dramatic Black Friday doorbuster event. Use retail signage, product stacks, bold arrows, countdown-ticket motifs, limited-stock labels and high-contrast CTA space. Make it ecommerce-ready, urgent and mobile-first.

description: Use a changing-room mirror layout where outfit options, confidence notes and 'try this with...' prompts surround the product.
whyItWorks: It recreates the in-store try-on moment and helps customers imagine themselves using the item.
ecomTieIn: Use styling notes, fit reassurance and cross-sell outfit pairings.
heroImagePrompt: Create a portrait hero image for an ecommerce email inspired by a fashion changing-room mirror. Use mirror framing, outfit option notes, styling prompts, product reflection, soft flash lighting and clear CTA space. Make it mobile-first, stylish and purchase-focused.
